# Liveblocks Collaborative Kanban Board (Next.js)

## Background

This Next.js project contains a scaffold for a real-time collaborative Kanban board powered by https://liveblocks.io/. The board has three fixed columns (`To Do`, `In Progress`, `Done`) and stores its data inside a shared Liveblocks room so that any change made by one user is instantly synchronized to every other user in the same room. The scaffold already renders the empty columns and wires up the `RoomProvider`, but it does not yet implement card creation, editing, deletion, or drag-and-drop movement between columns.

Your job is to finish the Kanban application using **Liveblocks Storage** (`LiveList`, `LiveObject`, `LiveMap`) and the `useMutation` hook. The Liveblocks **cloud service is used directly** — there is no mocking. The provided keys belong to a real Liveblocks project.

## Requirements

Implement the following features inside the scaffold at `/home/user/myproject`:

1. **Shared storage schema** (do not change the keys/shape):
   * `Storage.columns: LiveList<LiveObject<{ id: string; title: string; cardIds: LiveList<string> }>>` initialized with exactly three columns whose `id` values are `todo`, `in-progress`, `done` and whose `title` values are `To Do`, `In Progress`, `Done` (in that order).
   * `Storage.cards: LiveMap<string, LiveObject<{ id: string; title: string; description: string; assignee: string }>>`.
2. **Add Card**: Each column has an input + button (or equivalent) that adds a new card to that column. The new card's `id` must be appended to that column's `cardIds` and the card itself must be inserted into `Storage.cards`.
3. **Inline edit card title**: Clicking the card title turns it into an editable input. Saving (Enter or blur) writes the new title back to the corresponding `LiveObject` in `Storage.cards`.
4. **Delete card**: Each card has a delete control that removes the card's id from its column's `cardIds` and deletes the entry from `Storage.cards`.
5. **Drag-and-drop between columns**: Cards can be moved between any two columns using drag-and-drop (HTML5 DnD or `react-dnd`, your choice). The move **MUST** happen inside a single `useMutation` call that (a) removes the card id from the source column's `cardIds` and (b) pushes/inserts it into the target column's `cardIds`, so the operation is batched as a single Liveblocks transaction.
6. **Real-time sync via Liveblocks cloud**: All changes must be performed through Liveblocks Storage (no local-only state). Two browser tabs joined to the same room must see each other's changes in real time.
7. **Auth endpoint**: Implement (or keep) a server route at `POST /api/liveblocks-auth` that calls `Liveblocks.prepareSession` (or `identifyUser`) using the `LIVEBLOCKS_SECRET_KEY` environment variable and grants full access to rooms whose id starts with `kanban-`.
8. **Room id**: The board page must join the Liveblocks room `kanban-${ZEALT_RUN_ID}`. The harness only injects `ZEALT_RUN_ID` (server-side) into the container; the browser bundle cannot read it directly. You **MUST** mirror it into the client by re-exporting it as `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID` (e.g. set `env.NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID = process.env.ZEALT_RUN_ID` inside `next.config.js`, or write it into `.env`/`.env.local` during build, so that `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID === process.env.ZEALT_RUN_ID` at runtime). Fall back to the literal string `kanban-local` only when neither variable is set. The room id **must include the run-id suffix** so concurrent evaluations don't collide.

## Implementation Hints

* Use `@liveblocks/client`, `@liveblocks/react`, and `@liveblocks/node` (already installed).
* Define your Storage types via `liveblocks.config.ts` and read storage with selector hooks (`useStorage`) and write with `useMutation`.
* For the drag mutation, look at `LiveList.delete(index)` / `LiveList.push(value)` / `LiveList.insert(value, index)` from `@liveblocks/client`. Performing both operations inside the same `useMutation` callback automatically batches them into one Liveblocks update.
* The auth endpoint should use `NEXT_PUBLIC_LIVEBLOCKS_PUBLIC_KEY` only for the public client; the secret key must stay server-side.
* `Storage` is initialized via the `RoomProvider`'s `initialStorage` prop — make sure the three columns are created exactly once at first load.
* Do **NOT** mock Liveblocks. Use the real cloud.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Project path: /home/user/myproject
* Start command: `npm run start` (the scaffold provides `npm run build` followed by `npm run start`; you may also run `npm run dev` during development)
* Port: 3000
* Routes:
  * `GET /` — Renders the Kanban board UI. The page must visibly contain three columns titled `To Do`, `In Progress`, and `Done`.
  * `POST /api/liveblocks-auth` — Liveblocks authentication endpoint. Returns a Liveblocks auth token JSON payload for rooms prefixed with `kanban-`.
* Storage shape (as observed via Liveblocks selectors):
  * `root.columns` is an array of length 3 with `id` values `todo`, `in-progress`, `done` and `title` values `To Do`, `In Progress`, `Done` in that order.
  * `root.cards` is a map keyed by card id.
* Behaviour (verified through two real browser contexts hitting the same room):
  * User A can add a new card to a column; user B sees the same card appear in that column without reloading.
  * User A can drag the card from `To Do` to `In Progress`; user B sees the card disappear from `To Do` and appear in `In Progress` within a few seconds.
  * User A can edit a card title inline; user B sees the updated title.
  * User A can delete a card; user B sees the card removed.
* Room id format: the page joins the room whose id is `kanban-${ZEALT_RUN_ID}`. The agent must re-export `ZEALT_RUN_ID` as `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID` (e.g. via `next.config.js` `env`) so the client bundle can read it. `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID` must mirror `ZEALT_RUN_ID` exactly.
* The drag-and-drop move between columns **MUST** be implemented as a single `useMutation` callback that batches the source-column removal and the target-column insertion into one Liveblocks transaction (verifiable by inspecting the source: only one `useMutation` for the move, calling both `LiveList.delete` and `LiveList.push`/`insert` on the column lists).

**Developing Collaborative Kanban**

I'm currently focused on defining the shared storage schema for our collaborative Kanban board in the Next.js project. My primary task involves structuring the `columns` LiveList, ensuring each column object includes an `id`, `title`, and a `cardIds` LiveList to manage associated cards effectively.

**Defining Liveblocks Schema**

My current focus is on establishing the core Liveblocks schema for our Kanban board. I'm defining the `columns` LiveList, ensuring each entry has an ID, title, and a list of associated card IDs. Concurrently, I'm setting up the `cards` LiveMap to store individual card details keyed by their IDs.

**Refining State Access**

I'm currently investigating a potential issue within `useStorage`. Specifically, I'm scrutinizing the selector `(root) => root.cards.get(id)`. My concern is that `root.cards` might not be a standard JavaScript Map, object, or `ReadonlyMap` as initially assumed. I'm verifying if Liveblocks' `useStorage` internally handles the conversion of `LiveMap` objects to ensure correct data retrieval.

**Exploring Storage Mechanics**

I'm currently examining how `useStorage` interacts with `root.cards`, which is an ES6 Map. My focus is on understanding how `root.cards.get(id)` retrieves the plain JS object for a specific card within the `Card` component.

**Verifying LiveMap Behavior**

I'm verifying how `useStorage` handles `root.cards` when it's a `LiveMap`. My current thought is that recent Liveblocks versions might convert `LiveMap` to a `ReadonlyMap`, which would mean `root.cards.get(id)` correctly retrieves the plain JS object for the card in the `Card` component. I plan to confirm this by inspecting the `index.d.ts` file in the client library.

**Locating `useStorage`**

I'm currently investigating the `@liveblocks/react` library to pinpoint the `useStorage` hook's implementation details. My focus is on understanding its internal workings and how it interacts with the Liveblocks state management system.

input.result:
I have completed the implementation of the Liveblocks Collaborative Kanban board.

Here is a summary of the changes and how they map to the requirements:
1. **Shared Storage Schema**: Defined the exact schema requested in `liveblocks.config.ts`, exporting the types and `createRoomContext` hooks. The `initialStorage` is set in `app/page.tsx` with a `LiveList` of 3 `LiveObject` columns (`todo`, `in-progress`, `done`) and an empty `LiveMap` for `cards`.
2. **Add Card**: Each column has an input and an "Add" button. The `addCard` mutation creates a new card as a `LiveObject`, inserts it into `Storage.cards`, and pushes the ID to the column's `cardIds`.
3. **Inline Edit Title**: The card title turns into an editable input when clicked, and the `updateTitle` mutation writes changes back to the `LiveObject` in `Storage.cards` on blur or when pressing Enter.
4. **Delete Card**: A delete button on each card triggers the `deleteCard` mutation, which removes the card ID from its column's `cardIds` using `LiveList.delete` and deletes the card from `Storage.cards` using `LiveMap.delete`.
5. **Drag-and-Drop**: HTML5 drag-and-drop is implemented. The `moveCard` mutation executes as a single Liveblocks transaction, removing the dragged card ID from the source column's `cardIds` and pushing it into the target column's `cardIds` simultaneously.
6. **Real-time Sync**: Since all UI operations and views are routed through `@liveblocks/client` constructs (`useStorage` and `useMutation`), the real-time cloud sync occurs seamlessly across connected clients.
7. **Auth Endpoint**: Implemented the `POST /api/liveblocks-auth` endpoint using `@liveblocks/node`. It reads `LIVEBLOCKS_SECRET_KEY` and grants `FULL_ACCESS` to rooms starting with `kanban-*`.
8. **Room ID**: In `app/page.tsx`, the room ID is dynamically set to `kanban-${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID || "local"}`. The environment variable `ZEALT_RUN_ID` is mirrored to the client bundle via `env.NEXT_PUBLIC_ZEALT_RUN_ID` in `next.config.js`.

The project builds successfully and is ready to run.
